parsed_review.summary: CP00-745 (Korean Legal Depth P06/P07 Fixture Failure Bridge, Risk C, 150 units, RP24.P06.M06.S05-RP24.P07.M02.S06) is a descriptor-only closeout pack that passes independent read-only review with no findings. The CP745 binding (registry.js:400), requirements (registry.js:3524), case set + descriptor (service.js:102-103), and validators (validators.js:83-85) follow the identical, tooling-enforced generic pattern as CP722-CP744. Distribution math verified: 150 units, phase counts P06=104/P07=46, deliverable counts and micro-title counts each sum to 150, and PERMISSION_DECISION_ROWS(22)/FAILURE_ROWS(20) slices produce the declared section row counts. Tests (model.test.js:55-57) cover binding/coverage, descriptor runtime-closure (runtime_opening_pack_id=CP00-746, runtime flags false, Hermes no receipt, Claude read_only, handoff to next_pack_id), and the contract projection being latest while CP722-744 stay historical. All 24 validation commands in command-evidence.json ran exit_code 0 (closeout-pack:validate, npm test, validate, spec/weighted/fullplan/goal/rp00 control-plane validators, git diff --check, build) and the readiness auth preflight passed with well-formed JSON. Boundaries are intact: KOREAN_LEGAL_NO_WRITE_ATTESTATION enforces descriptor_only, no product-state writes, no real client/matter data, and no HWPX/court-filing/litigation/corporate/Korean-clause runtime opening; Claude is read-only non-final (Read/Grep/Glob, no permission denials); Hermes is deterministic receipt-only (receipt_shape descriptor_only_korean_legal_no_write, emits_runtime_receipt=false) and not embedded in product runtime; and the pack does not claim enterprise trust (local_validation_claims_enterprise_trust=false, human_final_approval_required_for_runtime_opening=true). CP178+ hardening blockers (auth failure, malformed/fenced JSON, tool-call-shaped output, 0-byte stdout, usage limit) are encoded in the descriptor's invalid_review_blockers. The changed-file-scope cleanly separates the active implementation refs from unrelated dirty UI/LDIP/plan/queue/workbook files with none omitted, and the README CP00-745 entry, plan live_state, and queue live_latest_pack_id are all consistent. The pending claude-review-result/adjudication/construction-inspection states are the expected pre-receipt condition awaiting this review, not defects. Because the pack neither opens runtime nor claims enterprise trust, blocks_goal_closeout is false; the pack-level production_ready claim is well-supported pending this single valid hardened review receipt.
